authoritative_sources.title: Segment 4.2 Effective Inventory Control
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIPS is the chartered professional body for procurement and supply. This segment is directly authoritative for inventory classification, replenishment controls, reorder logic, stockholding tradeoffs, and the terminology used in store replenishment recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory status, replenishment parameter, shortage, and reorder recommendation review
used_for: min-max, reorder, safety-stock, and stockout framing
boundary between recommendation and spend approval

authoritative_sources.title: Segment 7.2 Effective Expediting in Procurement and Supply
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIPS expediting guidance is authoritative for late supply, milestone tracking, and supplier follow-up concepts that shape grocery cut-order, lead-time, vendor-hold, and shortage escalation workflows without transferring supplier-management authority to this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: vendor constraint, late delivery, cut-order, and shortage escalation handling
used_for: late supply and vendor constraint evidence
handoff to procurement or supplier owners

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 1181 - Unit Pricing Guide - A Best Practice Approach to Unit Pricing (2025 Ed.)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes authoritative public unit-pricing guidance for retail store formats and e-commerce. It is directly authoritative for grocery replenishment when shelf capacity, package size, unit price, electronic shelf label, and e-commerce presentation determine customer-facing sale readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: shelf, package, unit-price, e-commerce availability, and promotion readiness review

authoritative_sources.title: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its order guidance is authoritative for customer-facing online or phone order timing, cancellation, and prompt refund expectations when grocery availability, substitutions, and replenishment shortages affect pickup or delivery orders.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: online order, customer commitment, cancellation, refund, and substitution-impact handling

authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the authoritative public model retail food safety reference used by many jurisdictions. It directly constrains grocery replenishment when receiving, storage, cold holding, shelf fill, return-to-sale, or customer substitution depends on food-safety control and disposition authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety receiving, storage, hold, saleability, and disposition boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official traceability FAQ is authoritative public guidance for traceability records, lot codes, receiving, shipping, and retrieval expectations for covered foods. It directly supports grocery replenishment evidence handling when lot, supplier, recall, or shipment records determine availability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability, recall pull, receiving, lot-code, and supplier record review

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes official recall, withdrawal, and safety alert records for FDA-regulated products including foods. These records are authoritative stop conditions for shelf replenishment, substitutions, promotion load-ins, and customer-facing availability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FDA-regulated recall, stop-sale, withdrawal, and customer-impact handling

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls and Public Health Alerts
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FSIS is the federal public health authority for meat, poultry, and certain egg product recalls. Its records are authoritative for grocery replenishment cases involving FSIS-regulated products, especially meat, poultry, deli, and prepared-food substitution or shelf-fill work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat, poultry, and egg product recall or public health alert handling

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail item identifiers and barcodes. Its specifications are authoritative for UPC, GTIN, barcode, lot, and data-carrier usage across grocery item master, POS, inventory, ordering, receiving, traceability, and recall workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item identity, barcode, lot, and record-linkage review
used_for: barcode, lot, POS, inventory, and recall alignment

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is an authoritative public control framework for documentation, monitoring, approvals, segregation of duties, and exception handling. It supports auditable replenishment evidence and helps define why the lane cannot approve its own spend, release, or policy exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence, control, approval, monitoring, and exception documentation

workflow_stages.name: Replenishment intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm banner, store, department, product category, channel, jurisdiction, customer impact, item identifiers, replenishment source, and tenant authority map before accepting the work.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Item identity and inventory-state validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, vendor item, pack, lot, item master, perpetual inventory, POS movement, cycle count, shelf audit, backroom, cooler, freezer, and shrink evidence before recommending action.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supply, vendor, and procurement constraint review
workflow_stages.description: Review warehouse, DSD, ASN, on-order, in-transit, fill-rate, cut-order, allocation, lead-time, MOQ, order multiple, vendor hold, emergency-buy, and approval constraints.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Shelf, service, promotion, and order impact assessment
workflow_stages.description: Map replenishment state to planogram facings, shelf capacity, ad load-in, e-commerce availability, pickup and delivery order status, substitution records, rain checks, customer service notes, pricing records, and refund triggers.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, recall, loss-prevention, and hold boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Keep recalled, stop-sale, unsafe, temperature-abused, identity-ambiguous, or loss-prevention-sensitive inventory out of saleable and substitutable availability until the authorized owner clears it.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Recommendation, escalation, and completion evidence
workflow_stages.description: Produce availability summaries, replenishment recommendations, shortage or vendor-risk escalations, and customer-impact handoff notes only when evidence supports the disposition and approval boundaries are explicit.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: item master, SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, vendor item, pack, case, lot, shelf-life, sell-by, department, category, and product attribute records

perpetual inventory, POS sales movement, cycle count, backroom count, shelf audit, planogram, facing, shelf capacity, cooler and freezer capacity, shrink, spoilage, damaged goods, and inventory adjustment records

purchase orders, store orders, replenishment recommendations, warehouse allocation records, DSD orders, ASNs, delivery confirmations, in-transit status, fill-rate reports, cut-order notices, lead-time records, MOQs, order multiples, and vendor constraints

promotion calendars, ad load-in instructions, e-commerce availability, OMS order status, substitution logs, pickup and delivery records, customer service tickets, CRM notes, rain checks, refund requests, and payment references

recall notices, stop-sale directives, FDA and FSIS alert records, food-safety holds, quality release notes, lot traceability records, supplier invoices, receiving logs, case label photos, and product withdrawal records
loss-prevention holds, fraud indicators, shrink investigations, repeated refund signals, vendor chargeback notes, scan-based trading records, approval matrices, and exception queues
systems_of_record: item master or product information management system

allowed: Classify grocery store replenishment requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Determine whether documented inventory, supply, vendor, warehouse, DSD, shelf, backroom, capacity, order, and customer conditions support a replenishment recommendation.
Prepare availability summaries, vendor or procurement action notes, shortage escalations, service summaries, customer-impact handoff notes, and completion evidence without overstating authority.
escalate: Required SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, item master, inventory, order, vendor, traceability, recall, hold, pricing, refund, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires supplier approval, spend approval, emergency-buy approval, supplier-risk acceptance, quality release, food-safety disposition, pricing override, refund exception, fraud adjudication, chargeback decision, or legal interpretation.
A tenant, franchise, supplier, category, jurisdictional, pricing, refund, substitution, safety, or recall rule materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, fraud, recall, safety, payment, or policy exception changes whether replenishment or availability can proceed.
refuse: Approve purchase spend, emergency buys, supplier onboarding, supplier substitutions, supplier-risk decisions, quality release, pricing overrides, refund exceptions, fraud findings, or legal interpretations.
Release recalled, stop-sale, unsafe, expired, held, temperature-abused, contaminated, or identity-ambiguous product into saleable or substitutable inventory.
Mark availability restored or replenishment complete without traceable inventory, order, shelf, vendor, customer, and safety evidence.

domain_failure_modes: perpetual inventory is treated as authoritative even though shelf audit, backroom count, POS movement, and on-order records conflict
a shortage is hidden or closed because a manager wants the aisle filled before a promotion
a vendor hold, missing ASN, cut order, or late delivery is converted into unauthorized emergency purchasing or supplier remediation
a substitution is approved despite missing category, pricing, customer notification, safety, or policy authority
lot, GTIN, UPC, case label, receiving, or recall records do not reconcile and the lane still restores customer-facing availability
recalled, stop-sale, temperature-abused, expired, or held food is placed back on shelf or allowed for pickup substitution
high-volume store intake causes missing evidence to be skipped across departments, channels, or stores
order, POS, payment, refund, and OMS records disagree and the lane promises a refund or service recovery outside authority
fraud-sensitive repeated refund or shrink signals are handled as routine replenishment corrections without loss-prevention routing
tenant-specific min-max, vendor, WIC-sensitive substitution, emergency-buy, or approval rules are ignored in favor of generic inventory advice

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, local, franchise, supplier, and tenant replenishment rules vary materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack requires tenant and jurisdiction context before applying replenishment, substitution, pricing, refund, WIC-sensitive category, recall, and emergency-buy rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public sources define baseline controls but not tenant-specific min-max, DSD, warehouse allocation, or approval thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The specialist may prepare recommendations and escalation packages but must not approve spend, supplier, quality, pricing, refund, safety, or policy exceptions without tenant authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FSMA traceability coverage depends on product category and compliance context.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack treats missing or disputed traceability coverage as a blocker requiring tenant product-category, supplier, lot, receiving, and jurisdiction context.

research_constraints_applied: Workflow claims are limited to evidence-backed store replenishment, availability, shortage, vendor-constraint, and customer-impact handling.

Scenario families explicitly test supply shortage, vendor or substitution authority, traceability gaps, order or payment mismatch, refund or policy exception, customer harm, fraud, high-volume intake, policy conflict, and tenant adaptation.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-specific variance is converted into required retrieval, refusal, or orchestrator-return behavior.
